user_symptom: Customers will experience increased latency. Query (and other) jobs will timeout, or fail.

Affected customers experienced elevated error rates and latency when connecting to Google APIs. Calls to these services would have experienced higher latency or elevated errors in the form of HTTP 502 response codes.

Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts. Affected customers were unable to connect to the service using the API in europe-west1. Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations. Service was unavailable in europe-west1 and europe-west4. Affected customers experienced probe creation request failures in europe-west1, europe-west4, and europe-west5. Affected customers were unable to create triggers in europe-west1. Affected customers experienced elevated errors in europe-west1. Customers might face connectivity issues. Cloud Run users are seeing increased HTTP 500s and authentication failures when trying to access apps.

Users experienced increased error rates across multiple GCP products. Specific symptoms included: Google Front End (GFE) preventing API requests from reaching services like Cloud Build and Cloud KMS; Cloud Firestore saw 80% of listen streams become disconnected; Cloud Logging was unavailable for viewing in the Cloud Console; Cloud Compute Engine requests saw an increase in 502 errors; and Cloud Spanner experienced elevated latency spikes which may have resulted in connection timeouts.

Customers may have experienced errors or incomplete monitoring data. Affected customers may see errors when trying to query their monitoring data. Customers may have also experienced false alerts during the impact window based on the underlying monitoring data.

We are experiencing an intermittent issue with Google Cloud infrastructure components, the issue manifests itself as periods of increased latency every 30 minutes. Cloud Networking traffic going through us-central1 and us-east1 shows increased latency.
APIs, Credentials, Monitoring, IAM and several other pages on the Cloud console are loading slowly and may ended up as a loading failure.
We are experiencing an issue with Cloud SQL.

We are currently experiencing an issue with Google Compute Engine customers experiencing elevated rates of packet loss in southamerica-east1-[a, b, c] due to the ongoing Cloud Networking issue beginning at Monday, 2020-10-19 13:05 US/Pacific.
Slow or stuck operations for zonal PD-Standard devices in us-west1-b.

Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts. Affected customers were unable to connect to the service using the API in europe-west1. Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations. Service was unavailable in europe-west1 and europe-west4. Affected customers experienced issues receiving notifications in asia-southeast1. Affected customers experienced probe creation request failures in europe-west1, europe-west4, and europe-west5. Affected customers experienced elevated errors in europe-west1.

Users of multiple Google Workspace and Google Cloud Platform services experienced elevated error rates, including HTTP 500 Internal Server Errors, generic unavailable errors, and error messages stating 'Policy Checks are unavailable'. Some services also experienced elevated latency and operation deadline expirations.

Significant packet loss on Cloud Interconnect in europe-west2 for some customers. We're seeing significant packet loss on Cloud Interconnect in europe-west2 for some customers. Diagnosis: Visible, near 100% packet loss on Cloud Interconnect.
Queries exhibited higher than normal latency. Customers were unable to fully utilize their reserved slots.
Affected customers may have experienced delayed log entries in Log Explorer or other export destinations for the duration of the incident.

Google Cloud customers using VMWare Engine experienced connectivity issues while using vSphere, vCenter and NSX in us-central1a zone. Affected Customers may have observed issues with network partitions and accessing workload VMs. Affected Customers may have experienced connectivity issues using vCenter, HCX, NSX Manager, and NSX-T Edge virtual machines. vSphere vCenter and NSX may not have been available for a short period of time in us-central1.
Cloud Logging logs-based metrics data points may be missing or delayed
Cloud logs might be unavailable for 25% of customers in global location. Logs won't be available, high latency when reading data.
Cloud Pub/Sub experiencing issues with seeing monitoring metric data especially delivery_latency_health_score. Other metrics data may also be affected.

Customers using CNAME chasing may experience an issue with Cloud DNS. Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer.

On Tuesday 21 March 2017, new connections to Cloud Pub/Sub experienced high latency leading to timeouts and elevated error rates for a duration of 95 minutes. On Tuesday 21 March 2017 from 21:08 to 22:43 US/Pacific, Cloud Pub/Sub publish, pull and ack methods experienced elevated latency, leading to timeouts.

Google Cloud Load Balancing (GCLB) experienced increased TLS handshake errors or connection terminations for HTTPS traffic served from load balancers in Western Europe. HTTP load balancing was returning errors for some users in Europe.
Users experiencing failure with file uploads. The case attachment feature has been disabled until the issue is resolved.

The issue with Cloud Logging `gcloud alpha logging tail` command and any direct calls to the Tailing API is believed to be affecting a very small number of projects. Customers may not be able to look at live logs by running "tail" commands.

Customers using Google Cloud Networking experienced a network traffic disruption in us-central1, us-east1, us-west1 regions. Some customers using Cloud Networking experienced severe traffic disruption for the two occurrences of the incident. Some cloud customers communicating outside the affected regions (including to the Internet) would have seen two periods of disruption.
GKE cluster pods restart when docker restarts.

Connections to and from VMs in us-east1-c and us-east1-d may fail. customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d.
preventing customers from creating or updating Support cases. Cloud Console pages that are nested under https://console.cloud.google.com/support may have also failed to load or update.

We are experiencing an issue creating and deleting Google Compute Engine instances. Attempts to create a new instance or to delete an instance fail with an error. As well, instances created may experience DNS propagation delays.
This may appear as timeouts, 5XX errors and potential authorization errors being returned from the service. We are investigating an elevated error rate in us-east1 for Cloud Storage Customers.

GKE clusters running node pools that use docker may experience containers restarting every time docker restarts. Customers may see containers restarting when docker daemon is restarted. Customers may see GKE Cluster pods restarting when Docker restarts.
Customers impacted by this issue would have experienced abnormal instance terminations for instances which were running in the impacted building.

Cloud SQL External Server or Database Migration Service (DMS) users who migrate to Cloud SQL for MySQL 5.7 with GTID replication using managed dumps may experience errors if they have insert/update/delete transactions during the dump phase. Furthermore, if updates were made to those jobs during the replication phase, the migration job may fail.

Google Cloud Networking experienced increased latency, packet loss, and service unavailable errors for traffic between regions and from Google to external endpoints including on-premises environments and the internet.
Connectivity issues in us-central1 resulting in failures and elevated latency.
Deplyoing or updating Cloud Load Balancers may be slow globally. Starting from 16:40 US/Pacific time, users attempting to update or deploy Cloud Load Balancers may experience delays or failures.

Customers using CNAME chasing may experience an issue with Cloud DNS. Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer.

Some services in us-east1-c and us-east1-d are failing, customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d. Connections to and from VMs in us-east1-c and us-east1-d may fail.
Anthos On-Prem customers unable to login to admin workstation.

Customers attempting to create github triggers using a Cloud Console URL that ends in their project id will see a "Failed to load" error with following message: "There was an error while loading /cloud-build/triggers?project= Please try again"

Customers may see containers restarting when docker daemon is restarted. Specific GKE Clusters upgraded from 1.19 to 1.20 (lower than 1.20.9-gke.2100) may experience pods restarting when Docker restarts.
Some customers may be experiencing connection failures in Docker workflow to GCLB, GCB, or Fastly destinations such as debian.org, github.com with a timeout error.
We are experiencing increased tail latency for predictions on Vertex AI Online Prediction. Users might experience higher latency.
We are investigating errors with creating and deleting Cloud Composer environments.
We are investigating an issue with GCE Networking in us-east4 region affecting GCE VMs, GKE, Cloud VPN and Cloud Private Interconnect resulting in network packet loss.
The issue with Google BigQuery API returning 503 errors has been resolved for all affected projects as of 1:30 US/Pacific. High error rate on multiple Google BigQuery APIs in the US region

We are investigating an issue with Google Container Engine (GKE) that is affecting node-pool creations in the following zones: asia-east1-a, asia-east1-c, asia-northeast1-a, asia-northeast1-b, asia-northeast1-c, asia-southeast1-a, asia-southeast1-b, europe-west1-b, europe-west1-c, us-central1-b, us-central1-f, us-east1-c, us-west1-a, us-west1-b and us-east1-d.
Our engineers have applied a mitigation and the error rate is now decreasing.
We experiencing delays in showing logging.
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
StackDriver Trace API is experiencing an elevated rate of request failures.
We are investigating an issue with Google Stackdriver Logging.
We are investigating an issue with Google Stackdriver Logging.
Cloud SQL First Generation instances unavailable in us-central1-a
We are investigating increased packet loss into and out of southamerica-east1. We are investigating an issue with network connectivity in southamerica-east1
The issue with Google Kubernetes Engine Node Pool creation through the Cloud Console UI has been resolved as of Friday, 2018-11-09 14:30 US/Pacific.
We identified an issue with Google Stackdriver alerting. Google Stackdriver Alerts not firing reliably.
Current data indicate that all GKE API requests in region europe-west4 are failing. GKE API unavailable in region europe-west4
Creation of new google cloud functions and update of current one are failing
We are investigating an issue with Google Cloud Networking.

We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Networking and Google Compute Engine in us-east4 that prevents the creation of GCE instances with external IP addresses attached. Allocation of new external IP addresses for GCE instance creation continues to be unavailable in us-east4.
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
The issue with Google Cloud Networking intermittent traffic disruption to and from us-central has been resolved for all affected users as of 2018-02-23 22:35 US/Pacific.
The Google Compute Engine service is experiencing delays in network programming in us-east1.
We are investigating timeouts with Google Cloud Functions deployments.
Users may see slow performance or intermittent errors.

Starting at Tuesday 27 June 2017 07:30 PST, Google Cloud Storage started experiencing degraded availability for some objects based in us-central1 buckets (Regional, Nearline, Coldline, Durable Reduced Availability) and US multi-region buckets. Between 08:00 and 18:00 PST error rate was ~3.5%, error rates have since decreased to 0.5%.

During the period 12:05 - 13:57 PDT, GCS requests originating in Europe experienced a 17% error rate. We are currently investigating elevated latency and error rates for Google Cloud Storage traffic transiting through Europe.
Stackdriver Uptime Monitoring experienced a period of unavailability.
We are experiencing an issue with packet loss for Google Cloud VPN, Cloud Interconnect, and Cloud Router.
We've received a report of an issue with Google App Engine as of Tuesday, 2018-07-17 12:26 US/Pacific.
The issue with Google Kubernetes Engine errors when viewing clusters on the dashboard has been resolved for all affected projects...
Stackdriver Logging Service Degraded
The issue with accessing Support Center has been resolved for all affected users. Support Center inaccessible.
Support Center inaccessible
Current data indicates traffic to other regions may see approximately 12% packet loss.
We are investigating an issue with Google Container Engine.
We are experiencing an intermittent issue with Cloud Storage showing inconsistent result for object listing for multi-regional buckets in US.
The rate of OAuth login errors is decreasing. OAuth login issues
The issue with Google Cloud Engine VM instances losing connectivity has been resolved for all affected users as of Friday, 2017-11-17 7:17am US/Pacific.
We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Support in which users are unable to create new support cases.
We are investigating loss for traffic to and from South America. The rate of errors is decreasing.
App Engine Admin-API experiencing high error rates

We are experiencing an issue with Cloud SQL V2, some instance maybe failing to create or HA instances may reports incorrect 'Unable to Failover' state beginning at Thursday, 2016-06-29 08:45 US/Pacific.
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
We are investigating an issue with Google Compute Engine.
Google BigQuery jobs failing with an "Internal Error" message.

We are investigating errors with Firebase Test Lab. We will be pausing all test execution until root cause is found and mitigated. All Firebase Test Lab submissions will be paused and queued for later execution
We are investigating errors with billing enabled projects causing console issues. Unable to browse GCS storage buckets in Google Console
High latency and failures on queries and deployments. High latency and elevated error rate

We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Storage service returning elevated error rates for requests in the US multi-region. Google Cloud Storage service is reporting a 1% error rate for all requests. Affected Google Cloud Functions customers may seen their deployments time out. Affected customers of Google Cloud Build were observable as "Build failed (internal error)".

We are still seeing errors on Cloud PubSub which is the underlying cause of the Cloud SQL admin operations failures. Current data indicates that approximately 60% of the Cloud SQL admin operations globally, including instance creation, are affected by the recent Cloud PubSub outage.
We've received a report of an issue with Stackdriver Monitoring
We are investigating an issue with Cloud PubSub.
We've received a report of an issue with Cloud Dataflow.

We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Storage service returning elevated error rates for requests in the US multi-region. Google Cloud Storage service is reporting a 1% error rate for all requests. Affected Google Cloud Functions customers may seen their deployments time out. Affected customers of Google Cloud Build were observable as "Build failed (internal error)".
Datastore experiencing elevated error rates
Users are unable to create new support cases.
We are experiencing an issue with Memcache availability beginning at November 6, 2017 at 12:30 pm US/Pacific.
Customers may experience intermittent elevated errors from the BigQuery API in europe-west2, especially TableService, DatasetService, and JobService operations.
We are investigating a potential issue with Cloud Dataproc in us-central1.
We've received a report of an issue with Google Cloud Function deployments seeing increased errors as of Tuesday, 2019-02-26 13:00 US/Pacific.
Intermittent errors for requests to the BigQuery API in europe-west2.
Customers without an organization are unable to create projects through the Enablement API and may receive an error message: "You may not have permissions to create projects in this organization".
We are experiencing an issue with packet loss for Google Cloud VPN, Cloud Interconnect, and Cloud Router beginning at Monday, 2018-10-22 13:42 US/Pacific.

Google Cloud Networking experienced loss of connectivity to external IP addresses located in us-east4 for a duration of 58 minutes. Google Compute Engine, Google Cloud VPN, and Google Cloud Network Load Balancers hosted in the us-east4 region experienced 100% packet loss from the internet and other GCP regions. Google Dedicated Interconnect Attachments located in us-east4 also experienced loss of connectivity.
Google Kubernetes Engine may clear certain add-ons from the configuration UI after a cluster upgrade.
Jobs using Dataflow Shuffle may fail or (when using Flexible Resource Scheduling) be queued indefinitely.
Issue affecting the ability to list projects and organizations
We are investigating an issue with Google Cloud Networking that is preventing the creation of new GCLB load balancers and updating of existing ones.

Customers affected by this issue may see an error similar to  the following:

ERROR: (gcloud.functions.deploy) OperationError: code=3, message=Build failed: # github.com/cloudevents/sdk-go/v2/extensions

src/github.com/cloudevents/sdk-go/v2/extensions/distributed_tracing_extension.go:161:3: cannot use span (type trace.Span) as type *trace.Span in return argument:

*trace.Span is pointer to interface, not interface; Error ID: 1093f764
We are experiencing an issue with Cloud Router monitoring data (metrics) missing. Visible decrease in Cloud Router data in Metrics Explorer.

All the components that require access to storage infrastructure may be affected by this issue in multiple regions. We are experiencing issues across multiple GCP products caused by an issue with Google storage Infrastructure

We are experiencing an intermittent issue with Google Compute Engine Managed Instance Group's Autoscaler... Autoscaling of instances in Managed Instance Groups may not occur due to instances being unreachable.
Jobs using Dataflow Shuffle may fail or (when using Flexible Resource Scheduling) be queued indefinitely.
Customers are not able to create new databases in Cloud Firestore. We are still seeing errors.
We are investigating elevated errors with Cloud Interconnect in Google Cloud Networking in us-west2. Customers peering with us-west2 may experience packet loss.
Google BigQuery jobs failing with an "Internal Error" message.
We experiencing delays in showing logging.
We are experiencing an issue with increased system lag in some Google Cloud Dataflow jobs. Some Dataflow jobs were stuck.
Affected customers may experience an elevated error rate (up to 100%) when submitting dataset insertion operations.
Google BigQuery users experiencing elevated latency and error rates in US multi-region.

Customers may experience increased latency or connection timeouts between GCP and other Cloud Providers. We are investigating reports of Connectivity issues between GCP regions us-east4 and us-east1 experiencing issues connecting to other Cloud Providers in similar geographies.

The Google Cloud Kubernetes Engine (GKE) service is experiencing an elevated error rate upon GKE cluster creation and deletion in all regions. Affected customers may experience timeout errors upon cluster creation and deletion.
We are experiencing an intermittent issue with Cloud Firestore beginning at Friday, 2020-12-04 12:09:08 US/Pacific.
We are investigating errors with Firebase Test Lab. All Firebase Test Lab submissions will be paused and queued for later execution.
Cloud SQL instances in europe-west2-a may be unreachable
We are investigating an issue with Google Kubernetes Engine node pool creation through Cloud Console UI.
We are investigating an issue with Google App Engine reduced URLFetch availability starting at 17:15pm US/Pacific.
Customers may observe their calico node pods failing to start and crash-looping indefinitely on GKE 1.24.7+ and 1.25.3+.

Cloud Memory Store users experienced sporadic levels of inability to create new instances... Affected customers were unable to create new instances, as the region field in the user interface (UI) could not be populated due to a metadata issue.
Cloud Functions Gen 2 customers are missing metrics and experiencing incorrect billing for their SKUs.

Google Compute Engine customers running “yum” or “dnf” commands in Red Hat instances experienced 403 forbidden responses when attempting to install packages. All Red Hat instances attempting to get updates encountered failures for the duration of the outage.
MemoryStore for Redis - instances update/export failures

Google Cloud Networking experienced intermittent packet loss for traffic between multiple cloud regions for 8 minutes. Customers may have experienced up to 40% packet loss between VMs in the following affected regions.
ssh fails with ERROR: (gcloud.compute.start-iap-tunnel) Error while connecting [4047: 'Failed to lookup instance']
Calico enabled GKE clusters’ pods may get stuck Terminating or Pending after upgrading to 1.22+.
Intermittent failures (ERROR: PERMISSION_DENIED: The caller does not have permission) when trying to list/describe the OAuth client via gCloud or Terraform
Customers may see failures while attempting to update/export their basic tier instances.

Vertex AI Online Prediction experienced increased error rates from 30% up to 100%. Affected customers may have experienced: all pre-existing Vertex models undeployed on Vertex AI Endpoints, empty responses when listing the deployed models, runtime exceptions and general errors on Predict and Explain requests, and quota failure when trying to re-deploy models.
Affected customers would have experienced elevated query latencies or in some cases query failures.

Affected customers experienced failures creating or editing GCE instances via the Google Cloud Console and may have received an error “Constraint constraints/compute.vmExternalIpAccess violated for project [project ID].“

Customers were unable to access their Cloud Volume Service (CVS) storage across GCP Regions. Customers may have faced issues with traffic routing In some cases where dynamic routes were not propagated appropriately on peered networks across regions. Some users are experiencing connectivity issues between VMs or with onprem.

Google Infrastructure Configuration Server operation requests failing. Customer's might experience following: 1) External HTTP/S Load Balancing, Cloud CDN - config changes will be accepted & fail to propagate 2) Cloud Armor rules may also be affected 3) Appengine flex customers may see apps fail to deploy 4) Traffic Director may see configs fail to propagate 5) VM instance group healthchecks & functionality like autoscaling/autohealing may be affected.

Customers may experience replication failure after maintenance on replica, if the master instance is busy with INSERTs/UPDATEs on tables with timestamp column and session timezone is set to other than SYSTEM.

Affected customers would have experienced increased packet loss and increased latency for the affected metros which would have caused delays and timeouts for the following services : - Cloud Load Balancing - Cloud Interconnect - Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) - Cloud NAT - Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
Customers may observe deployment failures and timeout errors.

Affected customers would not have been able to create new repositories in Artifact Registry, receiving a failure message. Customers were not able to create Composer environments, receiving errors. Customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets in Google Cloud Storage. Affected customers would have been unable to create new clusters in Google Kubernetes Engine and would see INTERNAL_ERROR when performing ClusterCreation.

Google Cloud App Engine customers might have experienced elevated errors while using the Secrets Manager. Customers might have experienced increased errors from apps or functions that use the Secrets Manager.
Cloud Composer v2 environments autoscaling might not work. Number of Airflow workers in the Composer environment won’t change.

Affected customers would have experienced increased packet loss and increased latency for the affected metros which would have caused delays and timeouts. Customers using Cloud Load Balancing would have experienced increased packet loss up to 60%. Cloud Interconnect customers would have experienced packet loss up to 80%. Customers using Virtual Private Cloud would have experienced packet loss of up to 50% and elevated network latency.
Customer configs propagation depending on the dos_cloud configs got delayed. Some impacted customers would not be able to make Cloud Armor config changes.

Cloud Function 2nd Generation deletes are erroring out in multiple regions. Delete function operations are erroring with following message: Operation Failed. Public Error Status: code: 5 message: Delete package operation for projects/[PROJECT_ID]/locations/[REGION]/repositories/gcf-artifacts/packages/[FUNCTION_ID]%2Fcustom-run has failed. Additional information: Requested entity was not found.
Cloud SQL for SQL Server is seeing a spike in instance creation failures globally.
Customers experienced a cloud networking disruption. All cloud customers communicating outside the region (including to the internet) would have seen two periods of disruption.

Affected customers would not have been able to create new repositories in the Asia multi-region. Customers would have received a failure message of “Request contains an invalid argument.” Affected customers in the Asia multi-region were not able to create Composer environments, receiving errors. Affected customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets in the Asia multi-region. Affected customers would have been unable to create new clusters in the affected regions.

An issue with Google Cloud Filestore resulted in all read-only API requests failing. ListInstances API requests failed with error code 429. This impacted read-only API access for customers globally. Console, gcloud and API access (List, GetOperation, etc.) calls failed during this time.
Customers may experience latency and errors with Composer creation.

We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Bigtable Metrics. CPU Load Metrics are impacted and could show the CPU Load as zero in the impacted locations. Customers who are periodically scaling their clusters based on the CPU Load Metrics will be operating on wrong data. Replication metrics are also affected. Customers may be missing data in Cloud Monitoring for the following metrics: bigtable.googleapis.com/cluster/cpu_load, bigtable.googleapis.com/replication/latency, bigtable.googleapis.com/replication/max_delay
Customers using the Secrets Manager are experiencing increased errors.
Retry Build operations may fail with an error and the build will not be retried
Cloud SQL for SQL Server is seeing a spike in instance creation failures globally.

service_name: Artifact Registry, Google Cloud Storage, Google Kubernetes Engine
user_symptom:
Customers will be getting IAM_BACKEND_INVALID_ARGUMENT in the Asian regions for Google Cloud Storage
Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR when performing ClusterCreation (GKE Cluster Server receives invalid_argument error when creating a GCS bucket as one step of cluster creation) for Google Kubernetes Engine
Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR Artifact Registry
503 error response can be observed for Google Cloud Functions. Cloud Scheduler jobs are failing due to 503 error from Google Cloud Functions.

Affected customers experienced failures creating or editing GCE instances via the Google Cloud Console and may have received an error “Constraint constraints/compute.instanceExternalIpAccess violated for project [project ID].“
Customers who are deploying GCFv2 functions and who have never enabled Cloud Run API before will observe failures with deployments.

Customers may see older jobs (finished more than 180 days ago) and jobs which are currently running disappear from the Console and API output. Jobs which are currently running will see data plane errors if the control plane job resource is deleted.
Datastore customers will experience increased errors and latency.

VMs using Local SSD are experiencing intermittent terminations. Customers using Local SSD on VMs could see the VM terminated unexpectedly, which shows in the console as an Instance Termination by Compute Engine.
Customers might have experienced issues while creating new forwarding rules for Global Layer 7 External load balancer or edit or delete existing ones.
Customers encountered error 429 when listing Cloud Filestore instances using API, gcloud CLI, and Google Cloud Console UI.
Customers may experience elevated latency for inter-region in multiple asia regions.
